hours_formula_detail: General nonprofit / community-service VOLUNTEERING uses the flat federal baseline of 80 hours/month (20 hours/week average). The SNAP-allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula applies ONLY to formal WORKFARE, which is a separate lane requiring a county-to-provider agreement and county attendance tracking. Two independent California sources state this explicitly: the Santa Clara County DEBS ABAWD handbook ('The requirement to work an average of 20 hours per week (80 hours per month) ... does not apply to workfare' and 'the number of required hours [for workfare] is determined by dividing the household's CalFresh allotment by the higher of the state, city, or county minimum wage'), and the LSNC calfresh.guide work-programs page (volunteer/community service = 20 hrs/week or 80 hrs/month; workfare = CalFresh amount divided by minimum wage). Because a recipient can self-initiate general community service at any qualifying nonprofit at the 80-hour standard WITHOUT the volunteer site being a registered/approved workfare provider, the general volunteer lane is classified federal_80, not workfare_formula or workfare_if_registered.
enforced_2026_detail: ABAWD time limits return statewide on June 1, 2026 (mid-FY2026). Seven counties hold individual area waivers Nov 1, 2025 - Oct 31, 2026 (Alpine, Colusa, Imperial, Merced, Monterey, Plumas, Tulare); the remaining 51 of California's 58 counties enforce starting June 1, 2026. The statewide waiver terminated Nov 1, 2025. Coded 'partial' because (a) statewide enforcement begins mid-FY2026 rather than Oct 1, 2025, and (b) seven counties remain waived for most of FY2026. Under HR1/OBBBA (2025), the affected age range expanded to adults through age 64 (previously 54), and the rules now reach parents whose youngest child is 14 or older.
minimum_wage_note: Set to 0 because the GENERAL volunteer lane is federal_80 and does not use a wage-based formula. For reference only: the California statewide minimum wage effective Jan 1, 2026 is $16.90/hour (up from $16.50 in 2025), per the CA Department of Industrial Relations. This $16.90 figure (or a higher applicable city/county local minimum wage) is what WOULD be used for the separate WORKFARE lane's allotment/min-wage divisor, where the county uses 'the higher of the state, city, or county minimum wage' and rounds down to the nearest hour.

regional_wages.label: California statewide minimum wage (2026; used only for the separate workfare lane, not the general volunteer lane)

qualifying_org_definition: Any nonprofit, community-service organization, or government agency where the ABAWD performs volunteer / community service work; no statewide approved-site list, no MOU, and no county pre-approval is required for the general community-service lane (the MOU / county-provider agreement requirement applies only to formal WORKFARE sites, not to general volunteer hours).
verification_instrument: CF 888 (CalFresh Volunteer Work Verification Form) — signed by an authorized representative of the volunteer organization, attesting to the recipient's volunteer hours. The form is OPTIONAL: per CalFresh Update 2025-07, the CF 888 is one of two new optional verification forms, and the county may accept other documentation (or a verbal statement from an org representative) in lieu of the form. Companion form CF 887 (CalFresh Medical Certification Form) verifies physical/mental unfitness to work.
submission_mechanism_detail: Recipient uploads the completed CF 888 to their BenefitsCal account (benefitscal.com), submits by mail, or delivers in person to the local county office.
submission_deadline: No fixed monthly filing deadline. Verification of volunteer/community-service hours is provided at the next recertification or periodic (semi-annual) report. Separately, a person meeting the requirement through work activities must report within 10 days if participation drops below the 20 hours/week (80 hours/month) average. A recipient may obtain CF 888 verification as often as they wish for personal records but is not required to submit to the county more frequently than at the scheduled reporting points.
remote_stance_detail: No California state (CDSS), county, or legal-aid source reviewed addresses whether remote / online / virtual volunteer hours qualify. Federal rules do not categorically exclude remote volunteering, but CA guidance is silent, so coded 'silent'. This is a product-guidance gap, not a classification factor.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No CA state or county source reviewed restricts volunteer host organizations to California-based entities. CDSS ABAWD policy, WCLP, LSNC calfresh.guide, the Santa Clara County handbook, and multiple county pages describe qualifying orgs only by TYPE (nonprofit, community-service org, government agency), with no in-state-location requirement. Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24 accepts any qualifying nonprofit regardless of location; absent an explicit CA restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be acceptable operationally. Coded 'silent' because no source affirmatively permits or forbids it.
required_prestep_detail: No EDP (Employment Development Plan), work-registration gate, county case-manager assignment, MOU, or county site pre-approval is required for the general volunteer / community-service lane. Work registration and the ABAWD work requirement are separate rule sets; the county only 'encourages' volunteer options at intake (informational, not gatekeeping). The MOU / agreement requirement applies solely to formal workfare sites.
geographic_waivers: Statewide waiver ended November 1, 2025. Seven counties hold individual area waivers November 1, 2025 - October 31, 2026: Alpine, Colusa, Imperial, Merced, Monterey, Plumas, and Tulare. Residents of these counties are not subject to the ABAWD time limit during the waiver window. The other 51 of California's 58 counties enforce beginning June 1, 2026.
county_variation_detail: California administers CalFresh at the county level (CalSAWS). Statewide policy establishes the clean 80-hour general volunteer lane and the optional CF 888, but county implementation, intake screening practices, and waiver status (7 waived counties) vary materially in practice.
conflicts_with_prior: abawd_enforced_2026: changed from 'yes' to 'partial'. The prior file coded statewide enforcement as 'yes', but FY2026 enforcement does not begin until June 1, 2026 (mid-FY2026) and seven counties remain waived through Oct 31, 2026 — 'partial' is the more accurate FY2026 coding.

geographic_waivers: prior file listed the seven waived counties and reconciled them as '51 other counties enforced'; this is corrected/clarified to note CA has 58 counties total (58 - 7 = 51) and that the statewide waiver ended Nov 1, 2025. Substance unchanged; framing tightened.

minimum_wage_used_in_formula_usd: prior file had no explicit minimum-wage field; this verified record sets it to 0 for the federal_80 general lane and records the 2026 CA statewide minimum wage ($16.90) only as workfare-lane reference.
unresolved: remote_stance: no CA source confirms whether remote/online/virtual volunteer hours count toward the 80-hour ABAWD requirement.
accepts_outofstate_nonprofit: no CA source affirmatively permits or forbids an out-of-state 501(c)(3) signing the CF 888; coded 'silent' on federal-default reasoning only.

CF 888 exact field list was not confirmed from the rendered PDF form itself; fields are reconstructed from multiple source descriptions (Section 1 recipient PII, Section 2 org/activity). EIN and notarization are NOT confirmed and were omitted conservatively.
Whether any individual county imposes an extra-statutory pre-step or local form for the volunteer lane in practice (county_variation_flag is true).
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22, FY2026 / OBBBA-HR1): Confirmed the prior file's federal_80 classification is CORRECT. The decisive evidence is that California cleanly separates two lanes: (A) general community-service / volunteer work at any nonprofit = flat 80 hours/month (20 hrs/week), self-initiated, no registration/MOU; and (B) formal WORKFARE = CalFresh allotment divided by the higher of state/city/county minimum wage, which requires a county-to-provider agreement and county attendance tracking. Two independent sources state lane (A) is 80 hrs and lane (B) is allotment/min-wage: the Santa Clara County DEBS ABAWD handbook (StsfygABAWDWkReq.htm) and the LSNC calfresh.guide work-programs page. Because Tended-style general nonprofit volunteering falls in lane (A) and any recipient can self-initiate it at the 80-hour standard without the site being a registered workfare provider, this is federal_80 (not workfare_formula and not workfare_if_registered). Enforcement re-coded 'partial' for FY2026: statewide start June 1, 2026; seven waived counties (Alpine, Colusa, Imperial, Merced, Monterey, Plumas, Tulare) through Oct 31, 2026; statewide waiver ended Nov 1, 2025. HR1/OBBBA expanded the ABAWD age ceiling to 64 and extended rules to parents whose youngest child is 14+. Verification instrument is the OPTIONAL CF 888 (CalFresh Volunteer Work Verification Form), signed by an org authorized representative; counties may accept other documentation or a verbal statement. Submission: BenefitsCal upload, mail, or in-person county office; cadence is at recertification/periodic report (with a 10-day mid-period report only if hours drop below the threshold). For reference, the CA 2026 statewide minimum wage is $16.90/hr (workfare-lane relevant only). Remaining gaps: remote_stance and out-of-state-nonprofit acceptance are both silent in CA sources; the CF 888 field list is reconstructed from descriptions, not the rendered PDF.
